solver: CPLEX (branch-and-cut, model A) and Gurobi (compact, model C)

models.description: Two-index arc formulation of the asymmetric multi-depot vehicle routing problem (and its MDATSP / MDAmTSP / ACLRP variants), solved by branch-and-cut with CPLEX Concert. The base model declared in createConstraints.cc holds only degree/flow/visit constraints plus small fixed subtour eliminations; the exponential family of capacitated subtour-elimination constraints and the paper's valid inequalities are separated dynamically inside the lazy- and user-cut callbacks (solve.cc) and via addOwnLazyConstraints.cc. This is the implementation accompanying Uit het Broek et al. (2020), Operations Research.

sets_indices.name: A
sets_indices.meaning: arc set, all ordered pairs (i,j) over V x V

sets_indices.name: S
sets_indices.meaning: node subset used in separated subtour/capacity cuts

parameters.name: q_i
parameters.meaning: demand of node i (0 for depots)

parameters.name: f_d
parameters.meaning: fixed opening cost of depot d (ACLRP variant only, when g_depotCost)

parameters.name: k(S)
parameters.meaning: minimum number of vehicles needed to serve node set S = ceil(sum_{i in S} q_i / Q)

decision_variables.name: s_d
decision_variables.meaning: depot-open indicator for depot d (ACLRP variant only, when g_depotCost; relaxed to continuous but pushed to {0,1} by linking constraints)

objective.expression_latex: \min \sum_{i \in V} \sum_{j \in V} c_{ij} x_{ij} + \sum_{d \in V_d} f_d s_d
objective.expression_plain: minimize total arc (routing) cost; the depot-cost term is added only in the ACLRP variant (g_depotCost == true)

constraints.name: depot_out_degree_at_most_one
constraints.expression_plain: each depot is left at most once (single tour per depot; santos/MDATSP setting)

constraints.name: depot_in_degree_at_most_one
constraints.expression_plain: each depot is entered at most once

constraints.name: depot_open_linking
constraints.indexed_over: d in V_d, j in V_c (only if g_depotCost / ACLRP)
constraints.expression_plain: an arc may leave depot d only if depot d is opened

constraints.name: flow_conservation
constraints.expression_plain: in-degree equals out-degree at every node

constraints.name: visit_each_customer
constraints.expression_plain: each customer has exactly one outgoing arc (is visited exactly once)

constraints.name: no_self_loops
constraints.indexed_over: all i in V (single aggregated equality)
constraints.expression_plain: no self-loop arcs are used

constraints.name: subtour_elim_size_2
constraints.expression_plain: eliminate 2-node subtours between two customers (statically added; comment marks it as mandatory)

constraints.name: depot_customer_size_2_elim
constraints.indexed_over: d in V_d, j in V_c (only if P_TYPE == MDAMTSP)
constraints.expression_plain: forbid a depot-customer-depot 2-node tour in the MDAmTSP variant

constraints.name: no_inter_depot_arcs
constraints.expression_plain: no arc may connect two depots

constraints.name: static_subtour_elim_size_3
constraints.indexed_over: i in V_c, and the 5 cheapest (j,k) triangles per i; added once before solve only if |V_c| <= 250
constraints.expression_plain: rounded-capacity subtour elimination over the cheapest 3-node sets, registered as CPLEX lazy constraints up front

constraints.name: static_subtour_elim_size_4
constraints.indexed_over: i in V_c, and the 5 cheapest (j,k,l) 4-cliques per i; added once before solve only if |V_c| <= 100
constraints.expression_plain: rounded-capacity subtour elimination over the cheapest 4-node sets, registered as CPLEX lazy constraints up front

instances.name: Santos benchmark (bgs-*.mdvrp)
instances.size: 10 files, ~2 KB-195 KB; encoded in filename bgs-{nCustomers}-{nDepots}-{idx}[a]: 100/20, 200/40, 300/60
instances.license: MIT (repository license; original benchmark by Bektas/Gouveia/Santos)

instances.name: Own generated instances
instances.format: custom; subfolders per problem type/asymmetry class
instances.size: 13 entries: ACLRP-{A,B,CRC}, MDACVRP-{A,B,CRC}, MDAmTSP-{A,B,CRC}, plus all_instances.zip

models.notes: BRANCH-AND-CUT: the base model in createConstraints.cc is intentionally weak; the routing is enforced by cuts separated dynamically. Cut families live in cpp/cuts/ and are dispatched from the CPLEX lazy callback CALLBACK_LAZY and user-cut callback CALLBACK_USER (cpp/modelA/solve.cc:88-253) through Cut::getCuts (cpp/cuts/getCuts.cc) and Cut::createIloRange (cpp/cuts/create_IloRange.cc:9-29, every cut emitted as expr <= rhs). Separated families (cpp/cuts/, registered in cpp/functions/make_cut_vector.cc:35-171): (1) Rounded-capacity / generalized-subtour cuts 'cap' (Cut_cap, lazy+user) using CVRPSEP CAPSEP_SeparateCapCuts; form x(S:S) <= |S| - k(S) i.e. sum_{i,j in S} x_{ij} <= |S| - ceil(sum_{i in S} q_i / Q) (cpp/cuts/Cut_cap/findCuts.cc:26-83). (2) Multi-depot subtour-elimination 'Laporte' / 'Lap (single)' via min-cut/Preflow over a per-component depot-augmented graph (cpp/cuts/laporte/findCuts.cc, cpp/cuts/laporte_single_customer/findCuts.cc); the single-customer case yields x_{d1,i}+x_{i,d2} <= 1. (3) The paper's signature valid inequalities D_k+/D_k- and their depot/length variants: 'DK+','DK-','DK_depot+','DK_depot-','DK_length+','DK_length-' (cpp/cuts/DK_plus, DK_min, DK_depot_plus, DK_depot_min, DK_length_plus, DK_length_min). (4) Standard VRP polyhedral cuts: 'comb' (Cut_comb), 'Tcomb' (Cut_Tcomb), 'T1comb' (Cut_T1comb), 'large star' (Cut_gen_large_multistar), 'hom star' (Cut_hom_multistar), 'framed_cap' (Cut_framed_cap), 'hypotour' (Cut_hypotour), 'cat' (Cut_cat). Their detailed lhs coefficient construction is in each family's findCuts.cc; only Cut_cap and the Laporte SECs construct an explicit closed-form expression transcribed above — the DK/comb/star families build coefficients algorithmically (graph/flow based) and were NOT reduced to a single static latex form (would be fabrication). Big-M d_M = 9999 is declared (modelA.h:41) but only used by the compact model C; model A uses no big-M. d_z/MTZ blocks at the bottom of createConstraints.cc (lines 181-430) are COMMENTED OUT and excluded. The same callback file also defines a primal heuristic callback (CALLBACK_HEUR) and an incumbent-logging callback (CALLBACK_INC) which are algorithmic, not part of the formulation.

models.description: Compact single-commodity-flow plus MTZ-style formulation of the same asymmetric multi-depot VRP, built with the Gurobi C++ API. Adds continuous load-flow variables z_{ij} and ordering/depot-assignment variables u_i (and unused y_i) so the model can be solved monolithically without dynamic cut separation. Used as the compact baseline against model A.

sets_indices.name: N_delta(i)
sets_indices.meaning: the d_delta cheapest outgoing customer arcs kept for customer i (others fixed to 0)
sets_indices.source_symbol: hash sorted by d_cost, threshold d_delta

parameters.name: c_{ij}
parameters.meaning: cost of arc (i,j) (set as objective coefficient on x_{ij})

parameters.name: Q
parameters.meaning: vehicle capacity (upper bound on z and y)

parameters.name: delta
parameters.meaning: neighbourhood size: number of cheapest customer arcs retained per customer

parameters.name: f_d
parameters.meaning: fixed depot opening cost (ACLRP variant, when g_depotCost)

decision_variables.name: x_{ij}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if arc (i,j) is traversed; objective coefficient c_{ij} attached at creation

decision_variables.name: z_{ij}
decision_variables.meaning: single-commodity flow (vehicle load) carried on arc (i,j)

decision_variables.name: u_i
decision_variables.meaning: depot-assignment / ordering variable used in MTZ-style subtour elimination and depot fixing

decision_variables.name: y_i
decision_variables.meaning: cumulative-load MTZ variable (declared; not referenced by any active constraint)

decision_variables.name: s_d
decision_variables.meaning: depot-open indicator (ACLRP variant only, when g_depotCost)

objective.expression_latex: \min \sum_{i \in V} \sum_{j \in V} c_{ij} x_{ij} + \sum_{d \in V_d} f_d s_d
objective.expression_plain: minimize total routing cost (+ depot-opening cost in ACLRP). Implemented implicitly: GRB_MINIMIZE is set in createObjective.cc and the objective coefficients are attached when the x and s variables are created (createVariables.cc:14,26).

constraints.name: neighbourhood_arc_fixing
constraints.indexed_over: i in V_c, arcs beyond the delta cheapest customer targets
constraints.expression_plain: fix to zero every customer-to-customer arc that is not among the delta cheapest outgoing arcs of i (search-space reduction)

constraints.name: depot_out_degree_at_most_one
constraints.expression_plain: each depot left at most once

constraints.name: depot_in_degree_at_most_one
constraints.expression_plain: each depot entered at most once

constraints.name: depot_open_linking
constraints.indexed_over: i in V_c, d in V_d (only if g_depotCost)
constraints.expression_plain: arc from depot d allowed only if d opened

constraints.name: flow_conservation_arcs
constraints.expression_plain: arc in-degree equals out-degree at every node

constraints.name: visit_each_customer
constraints.expression_plain: each customer visited exactly once

constraints.name: no_self_loops
constraints.indexed_over: all i in V (single aggregated equality)
constraints.expression_plain: no self-loop arcs

constraints.name: no_inter_depot_arcs
constraints.expression_plain: no arc between two depots

constraints.name: flow_demand_balance
constraints.expression_plain: single-commodity flow into customer j minus flow out equals j's demand (ARC1)

constraints.name: total_flow_from_depots
constraints.expression_plain: total flow leaving all depots equals total customer demand (ARC2)

constraints.name: flow_capacity_coupling
constraints.indexed_over: i in V, j in V_c
constraints.expression_plain: flow on arc (i,j) bounded by residual capacity and forced to 0 if arc unused (UB-ARC)

constraints.name: min_tour_load_mdamtsp
constraints.indexed_over: d in V_d, j in V_c (only if P_TYPE == MDAMTSP)
constraints.expression_plain: if depot d serves j, the tour starting there carries at least tourMin (MDAmTSP)

constraints.name: depot_assign_lb_in
constraints.expression_plain: u_i bounded below by the index of the depot whose arc enters i (constraint 1h)

constraints.name: depot_assign_lb_out
constraints.expression_plain: u_i bounded below by the index of the depot whose arc leaves i (constraint 1i)

constraints.name: depot_assign_ub_in
constraints.expression_plain: upper-bounds u_i consistent with the depot whose arc enters i (constraint 1j)

constraints.name: depot_assign_ub_out
constraints.expression_plain: upper-bounds u_i consistent with the depot whose arc leaves i (constraint 1k)

constraints.name: mtz_depot_consistency
constraints.expression_plain: MTZ-style big-M coupling forcing i and j adjacent on an arc to share a depot assignment (constraint 3.14)

models.notes: Compact Gurobi model. The objective is set implicitly: GRB_MINIMIZE in createObjective.cc, with c_{ij} attached as the addVar objective coefficient on x_{ij} (createVariables.cc:14) and f_d on s_d (createVariables.cc:26); z, u, y carry objective coefficient 0. y_i (cumulative-load MTZ variable, bounds [q_i,Q]) is declared (createVariables.cc:35-39) but no active constraint references it (the original MTZ-on-y block is the commented-out code in modelA/createConstraints.cc:248-298); recorded as a declared-but-unused variable rather than dropped. Depot index expression uses (d+1) because depots occupy node indices 0..nDepots-1. Big-M d_M = 9999 (modelC.h:27). The directory cpp/modelC/mycallbackcompact and setInitialSolution.cc provide a warm-start / callback harness, not part of the formulation. NOTE: this is the SAME underlying A-MDVRP problem as model A but a genuinely DIFFERENT formulation (compact flow+MTZ vs. branch-and-cut 2-index), so it is kept as a separate models[] entry per design decision D2.

extraction_notes: Two distinct formulations of the asymmetric multi-depot VRP from one repo: cpp/modelA (CPLEX Concert branch-and-cut, 2-index arc model with dynamic cut separation) and cpp/modelC (Gurobi C++ compact single-commodity-flow + MTZ). They are kept as separate models[] entries because they are mathematically different formulations, not merely two solver backends of one model. All constraints/objectives carry verified source line refs; code identifiers preserved in source_symbol. Paper DOI 10.1287/opre.2020.2033 is taken verbatim from README.md:3 (NOT inferred) - it is the Operations Research publication 'Asymmetric multi-depot vehicle routing problems: valid inequalities and a branch-and-cut algorithm' by Uit het Broek, Schrotenboer, Jargalsaikhan, Roodbergen, Coelho (2020). LAZY CUTS: model A's exponential subtour-elimination / capacity constraints and the paper's valid inequalities are NOT static; they are separated in the CPLEX lazy/user callbacks (modelA/solve.cc) via the polymorphic Cut hierarchy in cpp/cuts/ (16 families registered in functions/make_cut_vector.cc). Only the two cut families with an explicit closed-form lhs (Cut_cap rounded-capacity SEC, and the Laporte single-customer SEC) were transcribed as latex; the DK*, comb, multistar, hypotour, framed_cap and cat families build their coefficients algorithmically (graph/min-cut based) and were recorded by name + source path in the model-A notes rather than given a fabricated static expression. The fixed size-3/size-4 rounded-capacity subtour eliminations in addOwnLazyConstraints.cc ARE closed-form and are listed as constraints (they are registered up front, before solve). Large commented-out alternative formulations (MTZ/z-flow blocks in modelA/createConstraints.cc:181-430) were excluded as dead code. Instances referenced by path+size only (Santos benchmark .mdvrp files and an 'own' generated set); none inlined. d_y in model C is declared but unused - recorded honestly. No CPLEX/Gurobi build or run was performed.
